name: Fort Wainwright
tagline: The Army's Arctic post in interior Alaska, home of cold-weather and airborne soldiers near Fairbanks.
_note: Army Arctic post (Fairbanks, interior Alaska). Region flagged OCONUS for browse UX, but Alaska is a BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger state -> KEEPS full CONUS template (BAH MHA AK405 Fairbanks; realestate + schools_live feeds work). Home of 11th Airborne Division elements (Arctic Angels). Extreme cold/dark winters. Medical: Bassett Army Community Hospital. Facts verified from official sources (home.army.mil/wainwright, Bassett ACH, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Alaska is TRICARE West. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 99701 (Fairbanks).

data.Major unit: Elements of the 11th Airborne Division (the 'Arctic Angels')
data.Mission: Arctic and cold-weather operations and training
data.Climate: Subarctic: long, very cold winters and short, warm summers
data.Note: A true Alaska tour; remote and extreme but adventurous

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Fort Wainwright is the Army's interior-Alaska post, home to elements of the 11th Airborne Division, the 'Arctic Angels,' who specialize in Arctic and cold-weather operations. It's built for training and fighting in some of the harshest conditions on earth.
Right next to Fairbanks, it offers a true Alaska experience: dramatic seasons, the northern lights, and wide-open wilderness, balanced against long, dark, very cold winters that shape daily life.

sections.title: An Alaska Tour: Embrace the Extremes

data.subtitle: Fairbanks is remote and brutally cold in winter, which shapes everything. On-post housing is popular for the convenience; the real key is preparing for the climate.
intro: Interior Alaska is an adventure, but winter is no joke: deep cold, long darkness, and the need for engine block heaters and serious gear. Many families choose on-post housing for the short commute and easier winters.
Off post, Fairbanks and North Pole offer more housing options, and the cost of living is moderate, though goods and travel can be pricey given the remoteness.

recommendations.advice: On-post housing keeps the commute short and life simpler through the deep cold; it's a popular choice.
Fairbanks and nearby North Pole offer more housing, schools, and amenities a short drive out.
Budget for cold-weather gear, plug-in vehicles (block heaters), and higher heating costs; this is the biggest adjustment.
Northern lights, fishing, and the outdoors are incredible; embracing them is what makes the tour.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Fort Wainwright site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Fort Wainwright has privatized on-post family housing across several neighborhoods, popular for the convenience through Alaska's harsh winters.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools, including campuses serving the post. Fairbanks North Star Borough School District serves Fort Wainwright, Fairbanks, and North Pole.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

source.label: Fairbanks North Star Borough School District (official)

data.Care: A full Army hospital on post with primary, specialty, emergency, and inpatient services for the interior Alaska military community

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves. In Fairbanks, many families take on-post housing for easier winters, but here's the framework if you're weighing the local market.
rent: Your tour is short or your orders feel uncertain.
You prefer on-post housing for the short commute through the deep cold.
You'd rather not own in a remote, smaller market.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent if you go off post.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years and want to settle in Fairbanks.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
You're comfortable maintaining a home through extreme winters.
You've found a place and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, and many Wainwright families take on-post housing to simplify Alaska winters. If you buy, weigh maintenance, heating, and a remote resale market. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics, and an Alaska move adds climate-specific prep. Start early.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-post lodging before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: On-post housing is popular for easier winters; get on the list early and get the office's read on Fairbanks and North Pole.

steps.title: Winterize and register your vehicle
steps.text: Handle Alaska vehicle registration and base access, and prep for the cold: block heater, winter tires, and an emergency kit. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the Fairbanks North Star Borough School District early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Bassett Army Community Hospital.

data.q: Is Fort Wainwright an overseas tour?
data.a: It's in Alaska, a U.S. state, so it uses stateside pay tools like BAH (not OHA). But it's remote and extreme, so we group it under the OCONUS/Alaska section for browsing.
Interior Alaska has long, very cold, dark winters, often well below zero, plus short, bright summers. Cold-weather prep is the biggest adjustment.
Many take on-post housing for easier winters. Off post, Fairbanks and North Pole offer more options a short drive away.
Fort Wainwright is MHA AK405 (Fairbanks). Because it's a U.S. state, you get BAH; use the calculator above for your grade.
